WRITEUP
CAPESIZE
The market closed the week on a distinctly quieter and softer note, with the BCI 5TC retreating by $1,957 to finish at $42,151. After the gains seen mid-week, a natural correction emerged across both basins. In the Pacific, two miners were present, but with last done around $12.10 and offers reportedly in the upper $12s, resistance from charterers led to very subdued conditions and a lack of concluded fixtures. The C5 index edged down marginally by 0.055 to $12.020. The Atlantic followed a similar pattern. South Brazil and West Africa to China routes remained extremely quiet, with levels described as feeling a touch toppy, reflected in the C3 index also slipping by 0.055. While the North Atlantic remains tight, enquiry has thinned again, contributing to a muted finish and a slightly softer tone as the week came to a close.
Atlantic
Glencore fixed a Swissmarine TBN for 170,000/10 from Seven Islands to Qingdao fr 23/28 December at $35.00, further details have not come to light.
PANAMAX
The week ended on a softer tone in both the Atlantic and Asia. In the Atlantic, market sentiment remained weaker, with in-balanced fundamentals and a weaker fronthaul demand from the U.S. Gulf and East Coast. Asia mirrored this pace, with forward tonnage demand still viewed as healthy. However, charterers continued to exercise patience, showing little urgency to fix and thus keeping rate movement subdued. Short Indonesian runs are coming under pressure now as some owners are reluctant to fix to not expose themselves in open positions over the forthcoming holidays. Period activity was actively being reported today with the SDTR Faith (85 2022) open in the Far East 1/31 January fixing 11-13 months redelivery worldwide at $16,500 to Smart Gain. The BBG Nanning (81,702 2019) Caofeidian 06/07 December was reported to have fixed 6-8 months at mid-high $16,000\'s and the scrubber fitted Aomari (82,009 2014) open in Qinzhou 5 December fixed 5-8 months redelivery worldwide $17,000 to Norden. The P5TC average closed a further $237 down at $16,530.
Atlantic
The Moon (80,505 2012) fixed basis aps NC South America 17 December for a trip redelivery Skaw-Gibraltar at $17,000+$400,000 to Messrs Glencore.
Asia
The Pampero (93,275 2011) open in Port Dickson 08/11 December reportedly fixed and Indonesian stem into Korea with Panocean but a rate is yet to surface. Messrs Cambrian was rumoured to have fixed the Orchid Rising (76,466 2004) open Hong Kong 11 December for an Indonesian round voyage at $16,900.
SUPRAMAX
With a few events in both Athens and Geneva, unsurprisingly it was a rather subdued end to the week. Fundamentals in the Atlantic changed little, as the US Gulf remained relatively strong whilst the South Atlantic lacked fresh impetus. From Asia, similar to yesterday negative sentiment prevailed as the recent gains were eroded with limited fresh activity both north and south. Period activity appeared, with reports that the DSI Pollux (60,446 2015) open Mariveles 9/11 December was fixed for 12 - 14 months trading redelivery worldwide at $14,750 to Stone Shipping. Also it surfaced that the DSI Andromeda (60,309 2016) open Shanghai 7/8 December was fixed for 15 - 17 months redelivery worldwide at $14,600 to Western Bulk. The 11TC average closed down $59 to finish the week at $18,154.
HANDYSIZE
The week ended on a quiet note, with limited fresh information emerging from both the Atlantic and Asian basins. The BHSI remained at 841, while the 7TC average closed at $15,146, unchanged on yesterday. Market conditions across the Continent and Mediterranean remained largely unchanged, with rates holding steady for now. Despite a lack of reported fixtures, the US Gulf and South Atlantic continued to show upward momentum across all vessel sizes, with both owners and charterers gradually raising their ideas. In Asia, activity was also muted, with few fresh updates. Charterers were seen bidding slightly below last-done levels, contributing to a soft overall sentiment.
all the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
